findings.description: Import of synchronous existsSync in an Effect-based async codebase. Blocks the event loop on every call.
findings.worst_case: Event loop blocked for duration of each call (typically microseconds, but adds up in hot paths).

findings.description: Synchronous existence check in globalConfigFile(). Read-only — returns the path of the first found config file. No follow-up action assumes the file still exists.
findings.worst_case: Misleading return path if a config file is deleted between check and return, but the caller handles ENOENT gracefully via Effect.catch on subsequent loadFile.

findings.description: Classic TOCTOU race. Checks file does not exist synchronously, then writes asynchronously. Between the check and write, another process/agent could create the file. If the file is created externally, writeWithDirs overwrites it. The Effect.catch absorbs failures silently.
findings.worst_case: Configuration seed file ($schema pointer) overwrites user-created opencode.json if it was created in the race window. Data loss of user config. Mitigated by the fact that loadGlobal runs early at startup before user edits, and the catch silently swallows the write failure.

findings.description: TOCTOU between synchronous existsSync and dynamic import in Effect.promise. If the legacy config file is deleted between check and import, the import fails and is silently caught.
findings.worst_case: Legacy migration silently skipped. User loses migration from old config.toml to new config.json format. The catch(() => {}) hides the failure completely.

findings.pattern: if (existsSync(managedDir)) { for (...) { yield* merge(source, yield* loadFile(source)) } }
findings.description: Synchronous check of managed config directory existence, followed by async iteration over files inside. If the directory is removed between check and iteration, loadFile fails with ENOENT (propagates through Effect system). Managed directories (/Library/Managed Preferences, /etc/opencode) are system-controlled and unlikely to change.
findings.worst_case: Managed configuration not loaded if directory is removed mid-iteration. Low probability since machine-managed configs don't mutate during a session.

findings.description: Synchronous existsSync check before a Process.run('plutil', ...) operation. If the plist is deleted between check and Process.run, the plutil command fails with ENOENT and throws an unhandled error (no try/catch around Process.run).
findings.worst_case: Unhandled exception from plutil if the macOS managed preferences plist is deleted by MDM profile removal during check. In practice, managed plists are highly stable.

findings.pattern: existsSafe(plan) → used only for prompt text
findings.description: existsSafe check result is used solely to decide which prompt template text to inject. No subsequent file read or write depends on this boolean. If the file appears/disappears after the check, the prompt is slightly misleading (says plan exists when it doesn't, or vice versa).
findings.worst_case: Agent prompt says 'A plan file exists' when the file was just deleted, or says 'No plan file exists' when one was just created. Very low impact — just a prompt text choice.

findings.description: Same pattern as line 54. The existsSafe result drives prompt text only. The ensureDir is unconditional on the !exists path (run independently of the check result). No TOCTOU write risk.
findings.worst_case: Same as F07 — slightly misleading prompt text.

findings.pattern: existsSafe(localPath) → then existsSafe(.git) → then git config → then fs.remove(localPath)
findings.description: Multi-step TOCTOU race. The code checks if localPath exists, checks for .git subdirectory, reads git remote URL, then decides whether to remove the stale cache directory. Between the initial exists check and the fs.remove call, another process could create/modify/remove files in this cache directory.
findings.worst_case: Two concurrent ensure calls for the same repository: both pass the exists check before either removes, then one removes the other's fresh clone. The removed clone errors with CacheOperationError (caught). At worst, a redundant re-clone. No data loss outside the cache directory scope.

findings.description: TOCTOU between existence check and chmod. If the cached binary is deleted between check and chmod, spawnSync('chmod') throws ENOENT. The error propagates as an unhandled Effect failure. No retry or fallback.
findings.worst_case: Agent gets Effect defect/error when resolving a binary path. The resolve function fails entirely. The agent would need to retry the operation, which would then re-download the binary.

findings.description: Pure read-only check in the check() function. The existsSafe result is returned directly as a boolean. No follow-up file operation.
findings.worst_case: Stale boolean — could return true when the binary was just deleted, but the caller only uses this for informational reports.

findings.description: Pure read-only check in the info() function. Result drives status label ('cached' vs other). No follow-up file operation.
findings.worst_case: Stale status label in diagnostics output.

findings.description: TOCTOU between existsSafe check and the subsequent read operation (Effect.forEach at line ~130). If a matched instruction file (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md) is deleted between check and read, the read fails with ENOENT. No explicit catch around the read.
findings.worst_case: File read error when building instruction context for the agent. The Effect.gen catches it via the Effect.catch on findUp/read — but the specific read in Effect.forEach may propagate. Results in partial instructions shown to the agent.

findings.description: Returns a path if the file exists. No immediate follow-up. The caller may read the file later, creating a deferred TOCTOU. No lock.
findings.worst_case: Caller gets a path to a file that no longer exists, leading to ENOENT on subsequent read.

findings.description: HIGHEST SEVERITY FINDING. Classic TOCTOU race with synchronous-under-the-hood check. Filesystem.exists (line 11-12 of util/filesystem.ts) wraps existsSync (synchronous) inside an async function. Between the exists check and readText, the file can be deleted or modified. The exists call blocks the event loop. readText error is unhandled — no try/catch.
findings.worst_case: 1) File deleted between check and readText: readText throws ENOENT, unhandled → unhandled promise rejection in the ACP server. 2) File modified between check and readText: serves stale content to the ACP request handler. 3) Event loop blocked on every call because existsSync is synchronous. This runs in the ACP server's permission handler path — concurrent agent requests amplify the impact.

findings.description: TOCTOU between fs.access and fs.readFile wrapped in two separate Effect.promise calls. If the JSONL file is deleted between access check and read, fs.readFile throws ENOENT. The error propagates unhandled from readRecords. No file-level lock.
findings.worst_case: readRecords fails with ENOENT during a race window where the push-record file is being rotated/cleaned externally. The failure propagates as an unhandled Effect error, crashing the push-record read operation. Low probability since this file is only written by the same codebase.

recommendations.description: Replace the Filesystem.exists + Filesystem.readText pattern with a single fs.readFile that catches ENOENT. Use Effect's FileSystem.readFileString with .pipe(Effect.catchReason('PlatformError', 'NotFound', ...)) instead.

Replace existsSync + writeWithDirs with an atomic write-if-not-exists using O_CREAT | O_EXCL flags (fs.open with flag 'wx'). Or use Effect's writeFileString and catch the 'AlreadyExists' PlatformError.
Remove the existsSync guard entirely and let the dynamic import fail naturally with a catch. Or use Effect.promise directly without the sync check.
Add a Flock/per-repository mutex using the existing Flock utility (already imported in repository-cache.ts) around the ensure/clone/remove sequence.
Catch the chmod failure in binary/manager.ts and fall through to re-download rather than propagating the error.
Consolidate the access check and readFile into a single Effect.promise that reads and handles ENOENT internally. Or use Effect's readFileString with .pipe(catchReason).
Replace all imports of existsSync from 'fs' with the async AppFileSystem.existsSafe from the Effect service. This affects config.ts and managed.ts.

files_with_sync_imports.note: The Filesystem utility wraps sync calls in async functions. Used by acp/agent.ts line 243.
